exercises.prompt: Among monsters of Level (or Rank) 10 or higher, which have an ATK greater than the average ATK of all cards that share their race? Use a correlated subquery keyed on race and return name, race, and atk — ordered by race ascending, then atk descending, then name ascending. Consider only cards that have an ATK.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name, c.race, c.atk FROM cards c WHERE c.level >= 10 AND c.atk IS NOT NULL AND c.atk > (SELECT AVG(atk) FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.atk IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY c.race, c.atk DESC, c.name
hints: The inner AVG must be computed per race by referencing the outer card's race (c2.race = c.race) — that correlation is what makes it re-run per row.
WHERE c.level >= 10 AND c.atk IS NOT NULL AND c.atk > (SELECT AVG(atk) FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.atk IS NOT NULL), ORDER BY c.race, c.atk DESC, c.name.

```sql
SELECT c.name, c.race, c.atk FROM cards c WHERE c.level >= 10 AND c.atk IS NOT NULL AND c.atk > (SELECT AVG(atk) FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.atk IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY c.race, c.atk DESC, c.name
```

exercises.prompt: Which 'Blue-Eyes' archetype cards have been printed at least once? Use EXISTS with a correlated subquery against card_sets and return name, ordered by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name FROM cards c WHERE c.archetype = 'Blue-Eyes' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_sets cs WHERE cs.card_id = c.id) ORDER BY c.name
hints: EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_sets cs WHERE cs.card_id = c.id) is true when the card has at least one matching printing.
WHERE c.archetype = 'Blue-Eyes' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_sets cs WHERE cs.card_id = c.id), then ORDER BY c.name.

```sql
SELECT c.name FROM cards c WHERE c.archetype = 'Blue-Eyes' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_sets cs WHERE cs.card_id = c.id) ORDER BY c.name
```

exercises.prompt: Which 'Blue-Eyes' archetype cards have never been printed in any set? Use NOT EXISTS with a correlated subquery against card_sets and return name, ordered by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name FROM cards c WHERE c.archetype = 'Blue-Eyes' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_sets cs WHERE cs.card_id = c.id) ORDER BY c.name
hints: NOT EXISTS flips the test — keep the cards for which no matching printing exists.
WHERE c.archetype = 'Blue-Eyes' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_sets cs WHERE cs.card_id = c.id), then ORDER BY c.name.

```sql
SELECT c.name FROM cards c WHERE c.archetype = 'Blue-Eyes' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_sets cs WHERE cs.card_id = c.id) ORDER BY c.name
```

exercises.prompt: Which cards are Forbidden in the OCG format but have no entry at all on the TCG banlist? Use EXISTS for the OCG-Forbidden condition and NOT EXISTS for the missing TCG entry, and return name, ordered by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name FROM cards c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM banlist b WHERE b.card_id = c.id AND b.format = 'ocg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM banlist b2 WHERE b2.card_id = c.id AND b2.format = 'tcg') ORDER BY c.name
hints: Two correlated tests on banlist: one EXISTS that finds an OCG-Forbidden row, and one NOT EXISTS that requires no TCG row of any status.
WHERE EXISTS (... b.format = 'ocg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden') AND NOT EXISTS (... b2.format = 'tcg'), then ORDER BY c.name.

```sql
SELECT c.name FROM cards c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM banlist b WHERE b.card_id = c.id AND b.format = 'ocg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM banlist b2 WHERE b2.card_id = c.id AND b2.format = 'tcg') ORDER BY c.name
```

exercises.prompt: Which cards hold the highest ATK within their race (ties allowed)? Use a correlated subquery to compare each card's ATK to the maximum ATK of its race, and return name, race, and atk — ordered by race ascending, then name ascending. Consider only cards that have an ATK.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name, c.race, c.atk FROM cards c WHERE c.atk IS NOT NULL AND c.atk = (SELECT MAX(atk) FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.atk IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY c.race, c.name
hints: Correlate the inner MAX on race (c2.race = c.race) and keep cards whose atk equals that per-race maximum; equal ties all stay.
WHERE c.atk IS NOT NULL AND c.atk = (SELECT MAX(atk) FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.atk IS NOT NULL), ORDER BY c.race, c.name.

```sql
SELECT c.name, c.race, c.atk FROM cards c WHERE c.atk IS NOT NULL AND c.atk = (SELECT MAX(atk) FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.atk IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY c.race, c.name
```

exercises.prompt: Which cards are the sole highest-ATK card of their race — strictly greater than every other card sharing that race? Use NOT EXISTS to assert that no other same-race card has an ATK greater than or equal to this one, and return name, race, and atk — ordered by race ascending, then name ascending. Consider only cards that have an ATK.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name, c.race, c.atk FROM cards c WHERE c.atk IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.id <> c.id AND c2.atk >= c.atk) ORDER BY c.race, c.name
hints: The card is a sole maximum when no other card of the same race (c2.id <> c.id) reaches its ATK — express that with NOT EXISTS and c2.atk >= c.atk.
WHERE c.atk IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.id <> c.id AND c2.atk >= c.atk), ORDER BY c.race, c.name.

```sql
SELECT c.name, c.race, c.atk FROM cards c WHERE c.atk IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM cards c2 WHERE c2.race = c.race AND c2.id <> c.id AND c2.atk >= c.atk) ORDER BY c.race, c.name
```
